title: Connection of Certain Department of Labor Employees with the Case of Thomas J. Mooney
description: Congressional report into Labor Department contacts with the Mooney defense after the 1916 Preparedness Day bombing conviction.

title: Report on House Resolution 225 (Mooney Investigation)
description: House report on H. Res. 225 to investigate the Mooney–Billings prosecution.

title: Amnesty and Pardon for Political Prisoners — House Judiciary Hearings
description: Full transcript of 1921 hearings on amnesty for WWI-era Espionage Act prisoners; testimony from defense committees, Debs supporters, IWW counsel.
publisher: U.S. House Committee on the Judiciary (66th Cong.)

title: Amnesty to Prisoners Since the Armistice — S. Doc. 224
description: Wilson's Justice Department reply to the Senate listing every Espionage / Selective Service Act prisoner still confined after the armistice, with names, sentences, and prisons.

title: Amnesty to Prisoners Since the Armistice (Alternate Edition)
description: Companion edition of S. Doc. 224 — printed list of post-armistice federal political prisoners.

title: Freedom of Speech and the Espionage Act
description: Henry W. Taft pamphlet defending Espionage Act prosecutions, with case-by-case discussion of WWI political-speech convictions.
publisher: Henry W. Taft / National Civic Federation

title: Repealing Certain Provisions of Espionage Act — H. Rept. 870, 72d Cong.
description: House report on a bill to repeal Espionage Act sections under which WWI radicals were convicted.

title: Investigation Activities of the Department of Justice (Palmer Report on Radicalism)
description: Foundational Palmer Raids document — Attorney General Palmer's catalog of radical publications, organizations, and aliens targeted for arrest and deportation.
publisher: U.S. Attorney General A. Mitchell Palmer
collection: Library of Congress — Palmer Raids Era

title: Throttled! The Detection of the German and Anarchist Bomb Plotters
description: NYPD memoir of WWI-era anarchist and 'preparedness' bombing investigations including the Lexington Avenue and Preparedness Day cases.
publisher: Thomas J. Tunney / Small, Maynard & Co.
collection: Library of Congress — Palmer Raids Era

title: The Criminal Anarchy Law and on Suppressing the Advocacy of Crime
description: Legal commentary on New York's 1902 Criminal Anarchy Act passed after Czolgosz's execution — later used against Gitlow and Larkin.
collection: Library of Congress — Palmer Raids Era

title: Exclusion and Expulsion of Aliens of Anarchistic and Similar Classes — H. Rept. 504
description: House report on legislation broadening grounds for deporting anarchists — the legal scaffold for the Palmer Raids deportations.
collection: Library of Congress — Palmer Raids Era

title: The New Unionism
description: Survey of revolutionary syndicalism and the IWW published the year of the federal Chicago indictments.

title: General Defense Committee: Release All Class War Prisoners (Broadside)
description: IWW broadside demanding release of all 'class war prisoners' jailed under wartime Espionage and state criminal-syndicalism statutes; signed by William D. Haywood.
publisher: General Defense Committee of the IWW, Chicago

title: General Defense Committee: Will You Help? Now? (IWW Murder Appeal Broadside)
description: IWW broadside soliciting defense funds after the lynching of an IWW member; references Centralia and ongoing prosecutions.
publisher: General Defense Committee of the IWW, Chicago

title: Attention Workingmen! Great Mass Meeting Tonight at the Haymarket (English Broadside)
description: Original 4 May 1886 Haymarket meeting handbill introduced as the state's evidence in the 1886 anarchist trial.

title: Achtung, Arbeiter! Großartiges Massenmeeting am Haymarket (German Broadside)
description: German-language version of the 4 May 1886 Haymarket call, also used as trial evidence.

title: Caleb Powers — Appeal Circular (Washington, D.C., April 1906)
description: Fundraising circular describing Kentucky Secretary of State Caleb Powers as 'the noted Kentucky political prisoner' in the Goebel assassination case.

title: "Scottsboro Boys Must Not Die" — Mass Meeting Broadside
description: International Labor Defense broadside calling DC working people to a 1934 Scottsboro defense rally with Ruby Bates and Allan Taub.
publisher: International Labor Defense / Mt. Carmel Baptist Church

title: Debs v. United States, 249 U.S. 211 (1919)
description: Supreme Court opinion affirming Eugene Debs's Espionage Act conviction for the 1918 Canton, Ohio anti-war speech.

title: Abrams v. United States, 250 U.S. 616 (1919)
description: Supreme Court affirming Espionage Act convictions of Russian-Jewish anarchists for anti-intervention leaflets; Holmes's 'marketplace of ideas' dissent.

title: Berkman v. United States, 250 U.S. 114 (1919)
description: Per curiam affirming Alexander Berkman and Emma Goldman's Selective Service Act convictions.

title: Stilson v. United States, 250 U.S. 583 (1919)
description: Supreme Court affirming Espionage Act conviction of socialist anti-draft organizer David Stilson.

title: Schaefer v. United States, 251 U.S. 466 (1920)
description: Supreme Court affirming Espionage Act convictions of the editors of the Philadelphia Tageblatt German-language paper.

title: Pierce v. United States, 252 U.S. 239 (1920)
description: Supreme Court affirming Espionage Act conviction of socialists who distributed 'The Price We Pay' anti-war pamphlet.

title: Mooney v. Holohan, 294 U.S. 103 (1935)
description: Per curiam holding that the knowing use of perjured testimony violates due process — landmark Mooney habeas decision.

title: Gitlow v. New York, 268 U.S. 652 (1925)
description: Supreme Court affirming the criminal anarchy conviction of Communist Labor Party left-wing leader Benjamin Gitlow; first incorporation of First Amendment against states.

title: Whitney v. California, 274 U.S. 357 (1927)
description: Supreme Court affirming Anita Whitney's California criminal-syndicalism conviction for Communist Labor Party organizing; Brandeis concurrence.

title: Dennis v. United States, 341 U.S. 494 (1951)
description: Supreme Court affirming the Smith Act convictions of the 11 CPUSA national leaders in the Foley Square trial.

title: Yates v. United States, 354 U.S. 298 (1957)
description: Supreme Court narrowing the Smith Act and reversing convictions of California 'second-string' CP leaders.

title: Yates v. United States, 355 U.S. 66 (1957)
description: Companion Yates ruling on the contempt convictions.

title: Rosenberg v. United States, 346 U.S. 273 (1953)
description: Supreme Court vacating Douglas's stay of execution for Julius and Ethel Rosenberg.

title: Rosenberg v. United States, 346 U.S. 322 (1953)
description: Final Rosenberg denial of stay / rehearing — the executions were carried out the same day.

title: Rosenberg v. United States, 346 U.S. 324 (1953)
description: Companion 1953 Rosenberg per curiam.

title: Rosenberg v. Denno, 346 U.S. 271 (1952)
description: Earlier Rosenberg certiorari denial.

title: Brandenburg v. Ohio, 395 U.S. 444 (1969)
description: Supreme Court striking down the Ohio criminal-syndicalism statute historically used against IWW, CP, and anarchists.

title: The Life, Trial, and Execution of Captain John Brown
description: Contemporaneous 1859 compilation of trial transcript, correspondence, and execution of John Brown after the Harpers Ferry raid.
publisher: Robert M. De Witt, New York
collection: Library of Congress — John Brown / Harpers Ferry

title: A Voice from Harper's Ferry — Life and Death of the Last John Brown Hero (1873)
description: Memoir by Osborne P. Anderson, the only Black survivor of Brown's raid (1873 reprint).
collection: Library of Congress — John Brown / Harpers Ferry

title: A Voice from Harper's Ferry (1861 First Edition)
description: Osborne P. Anderson's first-edition account of the Harpers Ferry raid.
collection: Library of Congress — John Brown / Harpers Ferry

title: The Raid of John Brown at Harper's Ferry as I Saw It
description: Daniel R. Lee's eyewitness 50th-anniversary recollection of the raid.
collection: Library of Congress — John Brown / Harpers Ferry
